utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: um , i have high blood sugar . yeah , osteoarthritis , arterial fibrillation , and reflux .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i'm good . you ready to get started ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so , hannah is a , uh , 52-year-old female here for evaluation of a high blood sugar . so , hannah , what ha- what happened ? i heard you were in the emergency room with a high blood sugar .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes . so , i've been eating a ton of sweets recently . with the holidays it just feels like there is always something sweet available. whether i am at work and there are holiday gift baskets or clients bringing in treats or when i visit my family for the holidays and there is every baked good imaginable, there is always something sweet i can be eating. in the beginning i felt fine, but i have started to feel it after a couple of weeks, and this past week i just felt really light-headed and i could ... was seeing spots , really dizzy . so i went into the emergency room and they said i had a high blood sugar . um , so i wanted to come in and follow up with you to get that , um , get checked out .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . and how , how are you feeling now ? did they , did , did they treat you in the emergency room ? did they gi- they give you some iv fluids and things like that ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah . they gave me some fluids and they told me just to try to really cut out eating any extra sweets and get back into my normal routine and diet, but i am still staying with my family, and my mom and grandmother are big bakers so i think it will be really hard to get into a routine there so i just want to make sure i am nipping this high blood sugar thing in the bud.

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and i saw you went through a review of systems sheet with a nurse . i just want to ask you a few more questions from that standpoint . so you have had any fever or chills ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: any chest pain or shortness of breath ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: any joint pain or muscle aches ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i do have osteoarthritis though , so that , when i say , " no , " it's not , like , nothing out of the norm-

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: perfect . um , and then , i know that you have this history of a-fib , atrial fibrillation . have you felt your heart racing at all , recently ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: only when i'm really anxious . i , i feel it , um , pumping a little bit . but other than that i have n't .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . and how are you doing with your reflux ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i've been doing well . i've been taking the medication that you prescribed to me and that's been helping a lot .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , yeah . i see here you're on the protonix , 40 milligrams , once a day .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so that's , you're doing well with that ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . so let's go ahead and do a quick physical exam . so , listening to you , um , look , or looking at you , you appear in no distress . your thyroid is a little enlarged , but non-tender . there's no carotid bruits , your heart is in irregular rate and rhythm , and your lungs are clear . your abdomen is nice and soft , and you have no edema in your lower extremities . let's go ahead and look at some of your test results .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey , dragon ? show me the blood sugar . hey , dragon ? show me the blood glucose . hey , dragon ? show me the hemoglobin a1c . okay , so i see here that both your blood sugar and your hemoglobin a1c are elevated . so your blood sugars have probably been running high for probably a few months . okay ? um , let's go ahead and look at some of your other results . hey , dragon ? show me the diabetes labs . yeah . okay . yeah , so , um , my impression of you at this time , um , for your high blood sugars , that you do have this diagnosis of diabetes . now , we're not sure if it's type i or type ii . even patients in , you know , in their mid-twenties can develop type i diabetes , so we're gon na go off and , we're gon na go ahead and , and send some labs to work that up . hey , dragon ? order a complete metabolic panel . and i'd like to start you on metformin , 500 milligrams , twice a day . that will help keep your blood sugars down .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey , dragon ? order metformin , 500 milligrams , twice a day . and then if some of the autoimmune labs that come back show that you have an autoimmune disorder , meaning that you would have type i diabetes , then we would have to go ahead and put you on insulin . but i'm gon na hold off until we have those results , okay ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: but in the meantime , i really want you to avoid , uh , foods high in sugar . um , and the , the nurse will come in and she'll give you a glucometer and teach you how to do blood sugar testing . okay ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and then , from an atrial fibrillation standpoint , your heart , even though you're in atrial fibrillation right now , is in a good rate . and we'll just continue you on your metoprolol , 25 milligrams , twice a day . do you need a refill on that ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey , dragon ? order a refill on metoprolol , 25 milligrams , twice a day . and for your reflux , we'll just continue you on the protonix , okay ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: do you have any questions , hannah ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , so the nurse will come in and she'll go over all that stuff with you .

detail_list.summary: The patient is a 52-year-old female with a history of high blood sugar, osteoarthritis, atrial fibrillation, and reflux.
The doctor confirms the patient is a 52-year-old female presenting for evaluation of high blood sugar.
High blood sugar
The doctor asks the patient to describe the events leading to her emergency room visit for high blood sugar.
The patient reports dietary indiscretion with increased sweets during the holidays, followed by lightheadedness, dizziness, and visual spots, leading to an ER visit where she received IV fluids.
The doctor asks if the patient received treatment in the emergency room.
The patient confirms receiving IV fluids and instructions to improve her diet, but notes difficulty maintaining a routine while staying with family.
The doctor asks if the patient has experienced any fever or chills.
The patient denies fever or chills.
The doctor asks if the patient has experienced chest pain or shortness of breath.
The patient denies chest pain or shortness of breath.
The doctor asks if the patient has experienced abdominal pain.
The patient denies abdominal pain.
The doctor asks if the patient has experienced burning during urination.
The patient denies dysuria.
The doctor asks if the patient has experienced joint pain or muscle aches.
The patient denies joint pain or muscle aches.
The patient notes she has osteoarthritis but states her current symptoms are normal for her condition.
Atrial fibrillation
The doctor asks if the patient has felt her heart racing recently.
The patient reports heart racing only when anxious.
Reflux
The doctor asks how the patient is doing with her reflux.
The patient reports doing well and taking her prescribed medication.
The patient is taking Protonix 40 mg once daily.
Physical exam findings: patient in no distress, thyroid slightly enlarged but nontender, no carotid bruits, irregular heart rate and rhythm, clear lungs, soft abdomen, no lower extremity edema.
The patient's blood glucose and hemoglobin A1c are elevated, leading to a diagnosis of diabetes.
The doctor orders a complete metabolic panel and initiates metformin 500 mg twice daily.
The doctor orders metformin 500 mg twice daily.
The doctor advises the patient to avoid high-sugar foods and instructs the nurse to provide a glucometer and training.
The patient's atrial fibrillation is well-controlled at a good rate.
The patient continues metoprolol 25 mg twice daily.
The doctor asks if the patient needs a refill on metoprolol.
The patient confirms she needs a refill on metoprolol.
The doctor orders a refill for metoprolol 25 mg twice daily.
The doctor confirms continuing Protonix 40 mg daily for reflux.
The doctor asks if the patient has any further questions.
The patient has no further questions.
